title: Boat Rental in Turks Caicos: Top Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring Turks Caicos by boat offers access to secluded cays and vibrant coral reefs. Depart from Turtle Cove Marina in Providenciales, where a half-day motorboat rental typically ranges from 400 to 900 EUR. BoatHire24 connects you with local operators, allowing you to compare verified boat listings before booking.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Turks Caicos?

Half-day motorboat rentals start from 400 EUR, while full-day catamaran charters range between 1,200 and 2,500 EUR. Prices vary by vessel size, crew, and amenities. Shared tours from the Leeward Marina can cost as little as 150 EUR per person, including snorkelling equipment and lunch. You can compare verified boat listings in a few minutes.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Turks Caicos?

No licence is required for bareboat rentals, but operators often require a brief safety briefing and proof of boating experience. The operator will confirm exact requirements when you book. For crewed charters, the captain holds the relevant qualifications.

Where to set off in Turks Caicos

Several marinas and bays serve as ideal starting points for exploring the islands. Most rentals depart from Turtle Cove Marina or Leeward Marina in Providenciales. South Dock on Provo is another option, while Sapodilla Bay offers calm waters for smaller boats. On Grand Turk, the Cockburn Town Pier provides access to the western cays. For a trip to Salt Cay, the North Creek Marina is the main hub.

Which boat to rent

  1. Fairline Targa 43, a luxury sports cruiser for groups of up to 10, perfect for day trips to Iguana Cay. From 1,500 EUR per day. See the Fairline Targa 43 for hire on BoatHire24.
  2. Azimut 55S, a sleek flybridge for eight guests, ideal for sunset cruises along Grace Bay. From 2,800 EUR per half-day.
  3. Lagoon 450 Catamaran, a spacious catamaran for up to 12, best for snorkelling trips to West Caicos. From 2,000 EUR per day.
  4. Axopar 28, an open tender for 6, suitable for exploring the Mangrove Cay channels. From 600 EUR per half-day.

Cost breakdown for boat hire

  • Shared group tour (4 hours): 150, 250 EUR per person, including guide and snorkel gear.
  • Small motorboat (half-day): 400, 900 EUR, uncaptained, up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran (full-day): 1,200, 2,500 EUR, skipper and fuel included, for 12 guests.
  • Fully crewed yacht (daily): 3,000, 6,000 EUR, with chef and crew, for 8, 10 guests.

To explore all available options, simply use the boat search on BoatHire24 to filter by boat type, price, and location in Turks Caicos.

Season and local tips

The high season runs from December to April, when the trade winds are steady and seas calm. The prevailing easterly wind, known locally as the “Christmas wind,” can pick up in January and February.

For the best visibility, plan snorkelling trips in the morning before the wind freshens, especially around the barrier reef off Pine Cay. The water clarity can exceed 30 metres.

At a glance

  • Price band: 150, 6,000 EUR
  • Peak months: December to April
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 (depending on vessel)
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance: 12 nautical miles from Provo to West Caicos

Questions about boat rental in Turks Caicos

Can I book a boat for a week in Turks Caicos?

Yes, many operators offer multi-day charters, with weekly rates for catamarans starting at 8,000 EUR.

Are there restrictions on where I can anchor?

Yes, anchoring is prohibited in some marine parks such as the boundaries around Little Water Cay, but designated mooring buoys are provided.

What fuel costs should I expect for a day trip?

For a half-day trip on a 28-foot motorboat, fuel typically costs 100, 200 EUR, payable direct to the operator.

Can I fish from a rented boat?

Some charter boats are equipped for fishing, but a valid fishing licence is required and can be obtained online or from local tackle shops.

Now you have a clearer picture of boating in Turks Caicos. If you own a vessel and wish to reach a global audience, consider listing on BoatHire24 and rent out your boat to enthusiastic travellers. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Turks Caicos or list your own. Plan your 2026 escape with confidence.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Turks Caicos and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
